input.background: A particular random sample of $n$ observations can be used to calculate a sample proportion. The count of successes in the sample will vary according to a probability distribution. When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution. At the same time, the sampling distribution of proportion $\hat{p}$ in large samples will be Normally distributed.
input.question: When the sample is large, the number of successes will vary according to a Normal distribution with $\mu=(c)$ $\_\_\_\_$ and $\sigma=(d)$ $\_\_\_\_$. What are the mean and standard deviation of this Normal distribution?

sample_size.description: The size of the random sample used to calculate the sample proportion.

success_count.value: Count of successes in the sample
success_count.description: The number of successes observed in the sample, which follows a probability distribution.

sample_proportion.description: The proportion of successes in the sample, which follows a Normal distribution in large samples.

mean_of_normal_distribution.description: The mean of the Normal distribution for the number of successes in the sample.

std_dev_of_normal_distribution.description: The standard deviation of the Normal distribution for the number of successes in the sample.

population_proportion.description: The true proportion of successes in the population, used to calculate the mean and standard deviation of the Normal distribution.

meta_info.caption: Fill in the blanks.
meta_info.section: 8.3 SAMPLING BEHAVIOR OF A COUNT AND PROPORTION
